rulings.Case Title: ALVA RANSFORD VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Amended Notice Of Intent To Move For New Trial Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 657, Et. Seq.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 24, 2008 LINE 15. PLAINTIFF'S (AMENDED) NOTICE OF INTENT TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 657, ET SEQ. IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED NEITHER NEW FACTS NOR NEW BINDING LEGAL AUTHORITY. ON ITS FACE, THE 2007 AMENDEMENT IS LIMITED TO EVENTS OCCURRING AFTER JANUARY 18, 2002 AND THE EVENTS AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE OCCURRED LONG BEFORE THAT DATE. THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT DECISIONS ON FIELD PREEMPTION ARE CONTROLLING. SCHEIDING V GENERAL MOTORS (2000) 22 CAL 4TH 471 AND CARRILLO V A.C.F. INDUSTRIES, INC. (1999) 20 CAL 4TH 1158. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN DORNBLASER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendants American International Industries, Inc., Aryz Corp., Erx Corp., Glamour Industries Co. And Razy Properties
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 24, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL INDUSTRIES, ARYZ CORP., ERX CORP., GLAMOUR INDUSTRIES, CO. and RAZY PROPERTIES, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration IS DENIED BECAUSE IT IS DEFENDANT'S BURDEN TO PRESENT THE EVIDENCE NECESSARY TO PREVAIL ON ITS MOTION AND THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE 10-Q WAS UNAVAILABLE. FURTHERMORE, IF THE COURT WERE TO RECONSIDER ITS RULING, THE 10-Q FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT NESLEMUR REMAINED A FINANCIALLY VIABLE ENTITY. ALL THE 10-Q REFLECTS IS THAT NESLEMUR "HAS BEEN INACTIVE SINCE THE SALE OF ITS ASSETS IN 1987. THE ON-GOING VIABILITY OF KLEER-VU IS NOT RELEVANT TO THE FINANCIAL VIABILITY OF NESLEMUR ABSENT A SHOWING THAT KLEER-VU WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR NESLEMUR'S OBLIGATIONS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHILDREN'S DAY SCHOOL VS. ROBERT J MCCARTHY
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 24, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT ROBERT MCCARTHY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MANY OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT AND CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY DEMURRER, AN EXAMPLE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT ISSUE. EXHIBIT A TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SETS FORTH FACTS ALLEGING CONSIDERATION AND THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES ALLEGE AN ORAL CONTRACT. PROBATE CODE SECTION 19100, IN EFFECT AT THE TIME, HAD A THIRTY DAY TIME LIMITATION AFTER ACTUAL NOTICE. THE COMPLAINT USES THE PHRASE "ON OR ABOUT' AND THAT PHRASE HAS BEEN HELD TO BE TOO AMBIGUIOUS TO SUSTAIN A DEMURRER BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS: LIPTAK V DIANE APARTMENTS, INC. (1980) 109 CAL APP 3D 762,775 AND CHILDS V STATE (1983) 144 CAL APP 3D 155. =(302/PJM/PB)
